{"key_event":"Reform Act and rise of mass associations","reasoning":"Catholic emancipation, the 1832 Reform Act, municipal reform, abolitionism, unions, Chartism, and the Anti-Corn Law League made organized civil society broader and more effective, though labor repression and limited suffrage kept it below dominant. State capacity also rose through professional policing, factory inspection, centralized poor-law administration, civil registration, and more accountable municipal government, pushing Britain into the lower strong-state range.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":7.6,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":8.0}
